sections.paragraph:
to break, to break in pieces (compare פּוּר No. I). In Kal once inf. absol. פֹּור Isa 24:19 (although its form might be more correctly referred to פּוּר No. I.)

Hiphil הֵפֵר (in Pause הֵפַר Gen 7:14), inf. הָפֵר, with aff. הַפְרְכֶם Lev 26:15.-

(1) to break, always used figuratively, as a covenant, Lev 26:44 Isa 33:8 Eze 17:16 a law, Psa 119:126.

(2) to make void, as counsel, 2Sa 15:34 Psa 33:10, compare Job 5:12 Pro 15:22 Isa 44:25 to declare void, e.g. a vow, Num 30:9, 13 Num 30:13intrans. to be void, Ecc 12:5. Hence-

(3) to bring to nothing (vernichten), to take away, as piety, Job 15:4 any one’s right, Job 40:8 Psa 85:5, הָפֵר כַּעַסְךָ עִמָּנוּ “take away (avert) thy anger which is with us” (towards us).

Hophal הֻפַר to be made void, Isa 8:10 Jerem. 33:21 .

Poel פֹּורֵר to divide (the sea), Psa 74:13.

Hithpolel הִתְפֹּורֵר to be broken, cleft, i.e. to quake (as the earth), Isa 24:19.

Pilpel, to shake (compare Isa 24:19), Job 16:12.

II.

פרר

i.q. פָּרָא, פָּרָה to be borne swiftly, to run. Hence פַּרְפַּר, which see [also in Thes. פַּר, פָּרָה].
